2606.19522.title: REVEAL++: Differentiable Phenotypic Grouping for Vision-Language Retinal Modeling of Alzheimer's Disease Risk
2606.19522.paper_summary: The retina offers a noninvasive window into neurodegenerative disease, capturing subtle structural patterns associated with a risk of future cognitive decline. Vision-language alignment frameworks such as REVEAL have shown that pairing retinal fundus images with structured clinical risk narratives improves early prediction of Alzheimer's disease (AD). A key design choice in these approaches is the use of phenotypic grouping, where individuals with similar risk profiles are treated as multi-positive pairs during contrastive learning. However, existing methods operationalize phenotypic similarity as a discrete construct, relying on hard group assignments that impose rigid supervision and decouple group formation from representation learning. We propose a continuous formulation of phenotypic structure within contrastive learning. Rather than assigning samples to fixed clusters, we model inter-subject similarity as a differentiable weighting function derived from intra-modality embedding similarities in both retinal images and risk profiles. These weights define soft multi-positive relationships through a continuous aggregation operator, enabling graded supervision that reflects the spectrum nature of disease risk. We further introduce a soft-target contrastive objective that jointly learns cross-modal alignment and phenotypic structure in an end-to-end manner. Evaluated on UK Biobank retinal imaging data for incident AD prediction, the proposed framework consistently outperforms discrete group-based contrastive learning and standard vision-language baselines. By treating phenotypic similarity as a learnable, continuous signal rather than a fixed grouping rule, our approach provides a principled and robust foundation for population-scale neurodegenerative risk modeling from multi-modal retinal and clinical data.
2606.19522.paper_summary_zh: 視網膜提供了一個非侵入性的窗口，讓我們能夠觀察神經退行性疾病，捕捉與未來認知衰退風險相關的微妙結構模式。像 REVEAL 這樣的視覺-語言對齊框架已顯示，將視網膜底部影像與結構化的臨床風險敘述配對可以改善阿茲海默症 (AD) 的早期預測。這些方法中的一個關鍵設計選擇是使用表型分組，將具有相似風險輪廓的個體在對比學習中視為多正樣本對。然而，現有的方法將表型相似性操作化為一個離散的構造，依賴於硬性群組分配，這會施加僵化的監督並將群組形成與表示學習脫鉤。我們提出了一種在對比學習中連續化表型結構的公式。與其將樣本分配到固定的群集，我們將受試者之間的相似性建模為一個可微分的加權函數，該函數源自視網膜影像和風險輪廓中的模態內嵌入相似性。這些權重通過連續聚合運算符定義了柔性多正樣本關係，使得監督反映疾病風險的光譜特性。我們進一步引入了一個柔性目標對比目標，該目標以端到端的方式共同學習跨模態對齊和表型結構。在對 UK Biobank 的視網膜影像數據進行 AD 發生預測的評估中，所提出的框架始終優於基於離散群組的對比學習和標準視覺-語言基準。通過將表型相似性視為可學習的連續信號，而不是固定的分組規則，我們的方法為從多模態視網膜和臨床數據進行人口規模的神經退行性風險建模提供了原則性和穩健的基礎。
2606.19522.author: Ethan Elio Meidinger et.al.
2606.19522.authors: Ethan Elio Meidinger, Seowung Leem, Zeyun Zhao, Ruogu Fang
